The Senate reconciliation bill, known as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), extends many provisions of the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA) using a 'current policy' baseline. This bill was passed by the Senate and signed by President Trump on July 4, 2025. It is likely that the individual tax cuts were extended through this reconciliation bill.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025, introduces significant changes to the clean energy tax credit landscape established by the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA). The OBBBA harmonizes and enhances domestic content requirements for Production Tax Credits (45Y) and Investment Tax Credits (48E), making these requirements mandatory for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025. Specifically, the law revises domestic content percentages and introduces stricter rules for obtaining these credits, indicating that domestic content requirements are now a condition for receiving the 45Y Production Tax Credit. Given this, it is likely that the domestic content requirements became mandatory in 2025.

The domestic content requirements for the IRA's Investment Tax Credits (48E) are made mandatory by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act signed into law on July 4, 2025. This law revises domestic-content percentages for the clean electricity investment credit under section 48E, specifying required thresholds that must be met to qualify. Projects failing to meet these thresholds will receive a reduced credit, indicating that domestic-content compliance is now mandatory for full Investment Tax Credit eligibility.

The House of Representatives passed a reconciliation bill (H.R. 1) on May 22, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (Section 45X). The bill introduces new Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) restrictions that would require domestic materials or subcomponents for certain tax credits, taking effect under specific conditions. Although the bill is not final and may change before enactment, it makes it likely that domestic material requirements will be introduced for Section 45X credits.
